name: Check Every Pair
summary: Try each pair of indices until one pair adds up to the target.
visualizationIntent: init: show the array and initialize an empty result list. outer-loop: place i on the first number of the candidate pair. inner-loop: place j on each later number while keeping i fixed. check: highlight nums[i] and nums[j] as the compared pair and show their sum against target. return: when the sum matches target, color both cells as result and freeze with the answer indices.

lineExplanations.3: Create an empty result list that will hold the two answer indices once they are found.
lineExplanations.4: Choose the first index i of a possible pair.
lineExplanations.5: Store the value at index i so the pair comparison is easy to read.
lineExplanations.6: Choose the second index j after i so the same element is never reused and each pair is checked once.
lineExplanations.7: Store the value at index j as the second number in the candidate pair.
lineExplanations.8: Add the two selected values to see whether this pair reaches the target.
lineExplanations.9: Check whether the current pair's sum equals the target.
lineExplanations.10: Save the two matching indices as the result.
lineExplanations.11: Return the matching pair of indices immediately because the problem guarantees one valid answer.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper expected by the platform.
syntaxExplanations.2: def twoSum(self, nums, target): defines a method that receives the array nums and the integer target.
syntaxExplanations.3: result = [] creates an empty Python list.
syntaxExplanations.4: for i in range(len(nums)): loops i from 0 up to the last valid index of nums.
syntaxExplanations.5: first = nums[i] reads the array value stored at index i.
syntaxExplanations.6: for j in range(i + 1, len(nums)): loops j through every index after i.
syntaxExplanations.7: second = nums[j] reads the array value stored at index j.
syntaxExplanations.8: current_sum = first + second adds the two selected numbers and stores the total.
syntaxExplanations.9: if current_sum == target: runs the indented block only when the two numbers add to target.
syntaxExplanations.10: result = [i, j] builds a two-item list containing the answer indices.
syntaxExplanations.11: return result sends the answer back and stops the method.
